page: Microsoft Azure E4_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 cores with hyper-threading enabled. The x86_64 architecture processor operates at 2.3 GHz and includes a 50 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, alongside 102 GB of local storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this legacy hardware profile sits in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 10%, while Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores also align with this lower performance tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including large-block read and write operations, register in the bottom 10%, reflecting the older DDR4 architecture of the Xeon E5 v4 platform. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks show weak to poor performance, with Redis achieving 2.43 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the E4_v3 is its low compute and memory throughput relative to newer generation instances. However, it offers a high memory density per vCPU, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for legacy memory-bound workloads that do not require high CPU clock speeds or advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, basic web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that are compatible with older Intel Xeon architectures.
description: The Microsoft Azure E4_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs operating at 2.3 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage. Architecturally, it utilizes the x86_64 instruction set with a 50 MB L3 cache. Benchmark data indicates lower-tier performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests consistently placing in the bottom 10% to 25% of modern cloud instances. Consequently, the E4_v3 represents a legacy hardware profile where compute and memory throughput are traded off for a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with modest CPU requirements, such as small databases, caching layers, and legacy enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, designed for legacy memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage, suited for legacy database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized legacy virtual machine offering high memory density and local storage for modest, memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2.3 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per-core memory allocation
102 GB of local storage bundled with the instance
Lower-tier compute and memory bandwidth performance based on benchmark data
Best suited for legacy databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
